tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58192,"title":"[Communication Messages] Docs: Add Unified Messaging client documentation to README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` **Service directory:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #56911) by `@MaiLinhP` ## What Changed PR #56911 migrated `Azure.Communication.Messages` to the new TypeSpec emitter. As part of this migration, two new public client classes (`ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`) are now present in the package as part of the Unified Messaging feature (introduced in v1.3.0-beta.1). Additionally, message types added in v1.1.0 through v1.3.0-beta.2 (document/video/audio notification content, interactive messages, reaction messages, sticker messages, read receipts, typing indicators) remain undocumented in the README. ## Gaps Found - `ConversationAdministrationClient` is a public class with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts, no usage examples, and no authentication guidance - `ConversationThreadClient` is a public class (authenticated via `CommunicationTokenCredential`) with no documentation in README — not mentioned in Key concepts and no usage examples - "Key concepts" section only lists `NotificationMessagesClient` and is missing the two Conversation clients - `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, and `AudioNotificationContent` added in v1.1.0 have no examples (only `ImageNotificationContent` is shown) - Interactive Message, Reaction Message, and Sticker Message types added in v1.2.0-beta.1 are not documented - Read receipt and typing indicator (v1.3.0-beta.2) are not documented - "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" subsections are placed under "Key concepts" instead of under "Getting started" as required by the standard README template 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` — **edit** 1. Move the "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" content from "Key concepts" into "Getting started", after the "Prerequisites" subsection. 2. Expand the "Key concepts" section to describe all four main clients: - `NotificationMessagesClient` — sends one-way notification messages (WhatsApp / SMS) - `MessageTemplateClient` — retrieves message templates - `ConversationAdministrationClient` — creates and manages conversation threads (service-side, uses connection string or `AzureKeyCredential`) - `ConversationThreadClient` — sends and receives messages within a conversation thread (participant-side, uses `CommunicationTokenCredential`) 3. Add an "Authenticate the client" subsection for the Conversation clients. Example: ``` var connectionString = ""; ConversationAdministrationClient adminClient = new ConversationAdministrationClient(connectionString); ``` ```` var endpoint = new Uri(""); var communicationTokenCredential = new CommunicationTokenCredential(""); ConversationThreadClient threadClient = new ConversationThreadClient(endpoint, communicationTokenCredential); ``` 4. Add an "Examples" subsection for Unified Messaging (conversation operations), e.g., listing conversations and sending a conversation message. 5. Add examples for the media types missing from README: `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The repository uses `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject code from test files into README placeholders. Snippets should be written in a test file and referenced in the README. **Snippet test file path:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs` Create the test file with sample methods for: - Creating a `ConversationAdministrationClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate`) - Creating a `ConversationThreadClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate`) Wrap each block with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. Use `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` for test-only setup/cleanup. Reference `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example. In the README, add snippet placeholders at the appropriate locations: ```` ``` ``` (empty body — `Update-Snippets.ps1` fills this in) ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. The following are currently missing or misplaced: 1. **Getting started** - Install the package ✅ - Prerequisites ✅ - Authenticate the client — currently split between "Getting started" (only for NotificationMessagesClient/MessageTemplateClient) and "Key concepts" (Using statements). Consolidate here and add auth examples for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`. 2. **Key concepts** — needs expansion (see above) 3. **Examples** — needs new subsections for Conversation clients and additional media types 4. **Troubleshooting** ✅ 5. **Next steps** ✅ 6. **Contributing** ✅ Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — if any public types or members changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24483167154/agentic_workflow) · ● 373.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58192","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T23:18:56Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T23:18:56Z"}

Snippets should be written in a test file and referenced in the README. **Snippet test file path:** `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs` Create the test file with sample methods for: - Creating a `ConversationAdministrationClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationAdministrationClientCreate`) - Creating a `ConversationThreadClient` (snippet: `Azure_Communication_Messages_ConversationThreadClientCreate`) Wrap each block with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. Use `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` for test-only setup/cleanup. Reference `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` as a canonical example. In the README, add snippet placeholders at the appropriate locations: ```` ``` ``` (empty body — `Update-Snippets.ps1` fills this in) ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` must contain these sections in this order. The following are currently missing or misplaced: 1. **Getting started** - Install the package ✅ - Prerequisites ✅ - Authenticate the client — currently split between "Getting started" (only for NotificationMessagesClient/MessageTemplateClient) and "Key concepts" (Using statements). Consolidate here and add auth examples for `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient`. 2. **Key concepts** — needs expansion (see above) 3. **Examples** — needs new subsections for Conversation clients and additional media types 4. **Troubleshooting** ✅ 5. **Next steps** ✅ 6. **Contributing** ✅ Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` — check git diff to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 communication` — if any public types or members changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24483167154/agentic_workflow) · ● 373.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58192","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-15T23:18:56Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T23:18:56Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.Communication.Messages` README following the TypeSpec migration (PR #56911), specifically missing documentation for `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, and several new message types.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Communication.Messages` (v1.3.0-beta.1+)
**Affected API:** `ConversationAdministrationClient`, `ConversationThreadClient`, `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`, Interactive/Reaction/Sticker message types, read receipt, typing indicator
**Scenarios:**
- Two new public client classes added via Unified Messaging feature have no README documentation (no Key Concepts mention, no authentication guidance, no usage examples)
- Media types added in v1.1.0–v1.3.0-beta.2 are absent from README examples
- "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" are misplaced under "Key concepts" instead of "Getting started"

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` to document all new clients and message types, fix section placement, and add code snippet samples.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue created by automation, triggered by commit `d2dee264` (PR #56911). No runtime bug is reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review the current `sdk/communication/Azure.Communication.Messages/README.md` against the standard README template (use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as reference)
2. Add `ConversationAdministrationClient` and `ConversationThreadClient` to Key Concepts with authentication examples and usage samples
3. Add examples for `DocumentNotificationContent`, `VideoNotificationContent`, `AudioNotificationContent`, and the new v1.2.0–v1.3.0-beta.2 message types
4. Move "Using statements" and "Authenticate the client" from Key Concepts into Getting Started
5. Create snippet test file `tests/Samples/Sample_ConversationClients.cs` and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 communication` to inject snippets into README
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue is about an SDK client library's documentation (not management/provisioning/service-side behavior)
- **Service:** `Communication` — The package is `Azure.Communication.Messages` under `sdk/communication/`
- **Confidence:** High — Package name is explicitly stated, maps unambiguously to the "Communication" service label and "Client" category
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Communication` (line 214) — matched on the "Communication" service label; no more specific ServiceLabel entry exists for Communication Messages
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @Azure/azure-sdk-write-communication
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue is unassigned (ServiceOwners only, no AzureSdkOwners). "needs-team-attention" was NOT added because the issue does not carry the "customer-reported" label (it is bot-filed)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned CODEOWNERS bottom-to-top; `%Communication - Resource Manager %Mgmt` (line 948) requires "Mgmt" — no match; `%Communication` (line 214) matched with only ServiceOwners present
</details>
